definition: Japan provides an instructive but imperfect template for demographic decline in asset markets, with a generalizable sequencing (growth slowdown → rate suppression → currency pressure → fiscal expansion) but structural differences that limit direct extrapolation.
evidence: Japan's 30-year demographic decline provides the longest available analogue
Structural differences in reserve currency status, immigration, capital market depth, and policy frameworks limit direct extrapolation
open_questions: Which elements of the Japan sequence are generalizable vs. Japan-specific
Whether per-capita GDP performance (relatively strong in Japan) invalidates the doom narrative
